# **PROJECT 1: Small Business Network Subnet Design**

#### By: Cydrick James Cortel

### **Scenario:**

A small marketing company is setting up their office network. They have one Class C network (192.168.10.0/24) and need to divide it into separate subnets for different departments.

**Requirements:**

* Departments and user counts:  
* Sales Department: 50 users  
* Design Team: 25 users    
* Management: 10 users  
* IT Department: 5 users  
* Guest WiFi: 20 users

**Subnetting Table**

| Department name | Number of required hosts | Subnet mask  | Network address | First usable IP | Last usable IP | Broadcast address |
| :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- | :---- |
| Sales Department | 50 | /26 or 255.255.255.192 | 192.168.10.0 | 192.168.10.1 | 192.168.10.62 | 192.168.10.63 |
| Design Team | 25 | /27 or 255.255.255.224 | 192.168.10.64 | 192.168.10.65 | 192.168.10.94 | 192.168.10.95 |
| Guest WiFi | 20 | /27 or 255.255.255.224 | 192.168.10.96 | 192.168.10.97 | 192.168.10.126 | 192.168.10.127 |
| Management | 10 | /28 or 255.255.255.240 | 192.168.10.128 | 192.168.10.129 | 192.168.10.142 | 192.168.10.143 |
| IT Department | 5 | /27 or 255.255.255.224 | 192.168.10.144 | 192.168.10.145 | 192.168.10.175 | 192.168.10.176 |

**Network Diagram**

**![][image1]**

**Design Rationale:**

To calculate each subnet mask of each department, I sort them first according to which department need the highest number of host to optimize address allocation and minimize fragmentation

I also adjusted the subnet mask for the IT Department to accommodate for future expansion. IT Departments typically require extra IPs for servers, network tools, management interfaces, and testing equipment beyond just user workstations.

Each subnet was sized to accommodate current users plus 20-30% growth.

| Department | Network IP | Usable |
| ----- | ----- | ----- |
| Sales | 192.168.10.0/26 | 62 IPs (12 Remaining IPs) |
| Design | 192.168.10.64/27 | 30 IPs (5 Remaining IPs) |
| Guest | 192.168.10.96/27 | 30 IPs (10 Remaining IPs) |
| Management | 192.168.10.128/28 | 14 IPs (4 Remaining IPs) |
| IT Department | 192.168.10.144/27 | 30 IPs (25 Remaining IPs ) |

**Total Used**: 176 IPs out of 256  
**Remaining:** 80 IPs for future expansion

[image1]: <data:image/png;base64,>
